openingMessage: What can I do about it now? That's the only question that matters when something isn't right. The moment you stop blaming, you gain control. That's where leadership begins.
leadershipThought: Communicate early. Call leadership. Notify dispatch. Post in Breakroom. No surprises. Ever. That is how trust is built.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-30):
• Kelby Hammer + Kyle — Arrived at UNIVERSITY 6-48E #105H (8:43 AM)
• Jacob Hernandez — Arrived on Scharbauer (8:45 AM); arrived on Driver 3H, job started (afternoon); Bizzel Irvin conversion complete — atmospheric testing confirmed safe, below-grade conversion with Dvir arm utilized, Co Rep released, headed to shop (7:12 PM); arrived HP 616 (7:29 PM)
• Dermont Morgan — Heading to Tract 167 (8:50 AM)
• Louie Zarate — Cosmo phase 2: 9 AM pull H2S 3,000ppm / CO 3% (elevated readings)
• Stefanie Kirschke — Well shutting in for cement, not opening until 1 PM (11:35 AM); Ovintiv Tom Ranch released for the day, back at 7 AM Tuesday (6:20 PM)
• Raymundo "Ray" Solis — Arrived at University 3-21 (11:58 AM); done at University, heading to rig down at H&P 628 (7:18 PM)
• Marylou Guerra — Arrived at Stanton A (12:00 PM)
• Edward Carrillo — MT arrived Nobels 2304; permit live; HSMW all crews participating in safety meeting; beginning task (12:05 PM); Nobles 3202D rescheduled to later date, headed back (3:50 PM)
• Robert Wright — Job complete SDU Tract, heading back (12:10 PM)
• Michael Hutt — SM First Tee just opened #121 well (3:47 PM)
• Jordan Lemmons — Starting job (3:50 PM)
• James Stephens — Diamondback Mabee 13-24 1-1h finished for the day (6:30 PM)
• Caleb Smith — Shaw 13-8 #183 finished for the day (7:29 PM)

Carry-Forward:
• Stefanie Kirschke: Ovintiv Tom Ranch returning 7:00 AM Wednesday
• Jacob Hernandez: arrived HP 616 at 7:29 PM — job status not confirmed for Wednesday
• SM Energy Gipple 154H: 2,000ppm H2S — monitoring and escalation plan required before next crew visit
• Louie Zarate / Cosmo phase 2: H2S 3,000ppm + CO 3% — confirm supplied air protocol and re-check readings at start of Wednesday shift
lessonsLearned:
• SM Energy Gipple 154H — 2,000ppm H2S (Dennis Easley, 3:51 PM) — elevated H2S readings continue at this location; multi-week carry-forward
• Louie Zarate — Cosmo phase 2 elevated readings — H2S 3,000ppm + CO 3% at 9 AM pull; multi-gas hazard, confirm monitoring plan and supplied air protocol for next crew visit
• Nobles 3202D — job rescheduled — Edward Carrillo on standby, crane not present; rescheduled to later date; no safety incident but crew exposure time on standby at active location
exxonMoments:
Stop Work Authority Exercised — Unsafe Man Basket Operations (Line of Fire)
Man basket work is one of the highest-risk lift operations on an oil and gas site — Stop Work Authority before elevation is always the right call when anything about the setup looks wrong.

Exclusion Zone Breach — Third-Party Personnel (Near Miss) (Line of Fire)
When SimOps create unplanned traffic near your exclusion zone, proactive interception by all crew members — not just the safety tech — is what prevents a line-of-fire exposure.

Good communication between all crews, continuous HS monitoring, and the job (Operational Discipline)
Good communication between all crews, continuous HS monitoring, and the job was

Disconnect went smoothly with good teamwork and communication (Atmospheric Monitoring)
Disconnect went smoothly with good teamwork and communication. The job was

Roustabout crew member is fairly new (Operational Discipline)
Roustabout crew member is fairly new. Wasn't hitting a hammer union in the right

todaysChallenge: Find one person today and communicate something early — before they need it.
